subject: English
chapter: Verghese Kurien—I Too Had A Dream
source_pdf: Verghese Kurien—I Too Had A Dream.pdf

key_points: Verghese Kurien was an Indian engineer known as the Father of the White Revolution
He helped transform India into one of the largest milk-producing countries
Kurien worked with dairy farmers in Anand, Gujarat
He introduced the cooperative system to help farmers earn fair prices for milk
His efforts led to the success of dairy cooperatives like AMUL
examples: Kurien organized farmers into cooperatives so they could sell milk together
AMUL became a successful cooperative brand supporting dairy farmers
Operation Flood connected rural milk producers to urban markets
Milk production increased greatly because of the White Revolution
Farmers’ incomes improved due to the cooperative dairy movement
formulas_or_processes: Problem → Dairy farmers were exploited by middlemen
Action → Kurien introduced cooperative dairy systems
Development → Programs like Operation Flood expanded milk production
Result → Farmers gained fair income and India became a leading milk producer
exam_tips: Remember that Verghese Kurien is called the Father of the White Revolution
Understand how cooperative societies helped dairy farmers
Know the importance of AMUL and Operation Flood in dairy development
Focus on the main message: determination and cooperation can transform society
